source_text: Porter emphasizes that the essence of competitive strategy is about choosing a different set of activities to deliver unique value to customers. He further argues that companies must make deliberate trade-offs between competing positions. Southwest Airlines succeeded because it chose not to serve meals, not to offer assigned seats, and not to interline baggage.
summary_text: Porter argues that "the essence of competitive strategy is about choosing a different set of activities to deliver special value" to customers. Companies must make deliberate trade-offs. Southwest Airlines achieved success by choosing not to serve meals, not to offer assigned seats, and not to interline baggage.
